title: ShinyHunters, the elusive hacking network that started in France
subhead: Despite multiple arrests, numerous data breaches continue to be claimed under the ShinyHunters banner. Beyond its French roots, the group has now become a cybercriminal 'brand.'

title: At the G7, the US wants no mention of the words 'climate change'
Israel faces European backlash over Gaza flotilla detentions
How Italian authorities hamper the work of NGOs rescuing migrants
subhead: 'Migrants in the Mediterranean: The mechanics of silence' (3/4). Since Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni took office in 2022, organizations providing rescue services to migrants have faced increasing difficulty operating.

title: US indicts former Cuban president as pressure builds
subhead: An indictment unsealed in a federal court in Florida charged Raul Castro over the 1996 downing of two civilian planes manned by anti-Castro pilots. The charges came as Washington called on the communist-ruled island's people to embrace a 'new path.'

title: Jeremy D. Popkin, historian: 'Debates on slavery began decades before the French Revolution'
subhead: The American scholar traces the legal and intellectual history of abolitionism in France, showing how the question of slavery influenced the trajectories of many leading figures of the revolutionary movement.

title: 'France's president-PM diarchy is shaping the strategy of many 2027 hopefuls'
subhead: The role of prime minister has regained central importance since 2022 and the end of outright majorities in the Assemblée Nationale, observes Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

title: US needs to 'put its footprint back on Greenland,' says envoy Landry

'Heat strokes are part of the job': In Delhi, construction workers endure 43°C heat
Historic buildings in Normandy's Honfleur on verge of collapse
Robin Campillo, filmmaker: 'I don't see how a blacklist of French cinema could be enforced. Would it even be legal?'

Trump sinks lower into nepotism with fund to compensate allies
subhead: Acting attorney general Todd Blanche announced the creation of a fund totaling $1.776 billion for Trump's prosecuted allies and established a 'permanent' ban on tax audits into the president and his family.

title: As Israeli elections approach, the opposition focuses on rejecting Netanyahu rather than defining peace
subhead: Even as the current prime minister's coalition appears to be in deep trouble, most of his rivals have not dared to break with his nationalist rhetoric, and seem trapped in a conceptual dead end when it comes to coexistence with Palestinians – whether Israeli citizens, residents of Gaza, or the West Bank.

title: Canal+ 'blacklist' threat fuels tensions in the French film industry
subhead: The number of signatories to the anti-Bolloré open letter continues to grow as the CNC, France's national film board, tries to ease tensions and many producers, actors and directors defended Canal+'s work.
byline: Boris Bastide,  Clarisse Fabre and  Nicole Vulser
